paper.title_en: Designing Josephson Traveling-Wave Parametric Amplifiers: EM-Circuit Cosimulation and Experimental Validation
paper.abstract_en: The development of large-scale superconducting quantum computers critically depends on the accurate readout of qubit states. To enable this, cryogenic parametric amplifiers have been proposed and developed, and have since found widespread use in small-signal detection applications. Among various amplifier architectures, Josephson traveling-wave parametric amplifiers (JTWPAs) are widely adopted due to their broad bandwidth, high saturation power, and compatibility with superconducting qubits. In this work, we present a design workflow of JTWPAs based on a cosimulation approach combining electromagnetic (EM) and circuit-level simulation. We apply this method to a uniform JTWPA, comparing our simulation results with experimental data. To mitigate the challenge of constructing lumped-element circuit models usually used in the conventional simulation method, we directly perform full EM analysis of the device to accurately determine its linear response. The extracted S-parameters are then fed into a nonlinear harmonic balance simulator with Josephson junctions modeled as circuit elements. By doing so, the linear and gain properties of the JTWPA are extracted. Different factors that impact device performance including gain compression, phase matching, and fabrication imperfections are further investigated and modeled. A good agreement between simulation and measurements is identified, establishing the effectiveness of our approach in designing quantum parametric amplifiers.
